notes: All checks pass. The claim and modified_text appear verbatim in the report. The citation_as_written matches the report citation exactly. The hallucination_type total_citation_fabrication correctly maps to expected_verdict cannot_verify. The empty original_text is consistent with a fully fabricated source where no real document exists. The type label is correct: the source URL is a constructed fake path (fake-doc.htm), the exhibit Exhibit 99.2 'FY2026 Outlook Statement' does not exist as a standard 10-K exhibit, and the specific 40% Azure FY2026 guidance with a $25B commitment is not found in any real Microsoft 10-K. Subtlety rating 3 is plausible — Microsoft's real CIK (789019) and realistic filing metadata lend surface credibility, but the fake URL and invented exhibit name would fail even a cursory SEC EDGAR lookup.
haiku_reason: The citation format is self-evidently suspicious. The claim attributes specific revenue growth guidance ("at least 40%") to "Exhibit 99.2 'FY2026 Outlook Statement'" in a 10-K filing for a completed fiscal year. This is an unusual structure — Microsoft's forward-looking guidance typically appears in earnings call transcripts and investor presentations, not as exhibits in historical 10-K filings. The precise framing of a revenue growth target backed by a specific capex figure in a formal SEC exhibit is atypical and suggests the citation may be fabricated or misattributed.
